description: Secrets/config management, vulnerability scanning, and application security testing services.

entries.name: Doppler
entries.summary: Universal secrets manager for application secrets and config, syncing to most cloud providers and CI tools.
entries.freeTier: Free for unlimited users with basic access controls and config syncing.
entries.paidFrom: Team plan billed per seat for advanced access controls.

entries.name: HashiCorp Vault (HCP Vault)
entries.summary: Industry-standard secrets management and encryption-as-a-service, available self-hosted (free OSS) or as managed HCP Vault.
entries.freeTier: Self-hosted OSS edition fully free; HCP Vault has a limited free development tier.
entries.paidFrom: HCP Vault plans billed hourly on cluster size.

entries.name: Infisical
entries.summary: Open-source secrets management platform with a managed cloud option, designed as a Doppler/Vault alternative.
entries.freeTier: Self-hosted free; cloud free tier for small teams.

entries.name: 1Password Developer Tools (Secrets Automation)
entries.summary: Secrets automation tooling (CLI, SDKs, Connect server) built on top of 1Password vaults for CI/CD pipelines.
entries.freeTier: Free trial for teams; secrets automation included with paid Business plans.

entries.name: Snyk (security scanning)
entries.summary: Scans code, open-source dependencies, containers, and infrastructure-as-code for vulnerabilities.
entries.freeTier: Free tier with limited monthly tests across products.

entries.name: Trivy
entries.summary: Open-source, all-in-one vulnerability and misconfiguration scanner for containers, IaC, and filesystems.

entries.name: GitGuardian
entries.summary: Detects hardcoded secrets and credentials leaked in source code, commit history, and CI logs.
entries.freeTier: Free tier for individual developers and small teams.
entries.paidFrom: Business plan billed per developer seat.

entries.name: Have I Been Pwned API
entries.summary: API for checking whether an email/password has appeared in known data breaches.
entries.freeTier: N/A — the API requires a paid subscription key as of recent pricing changes.
entries.notes: The public website lookup remains free for individuals; the API itself requires a paid key.
